steps.title: Identify which works the notes call most famous
steps.content: The notes explicitly identify C.M. Forster's most famous novels as A Room with a View (1908) and Howards End (1910). To provide a specific example of one of his most famous works, the answer must reference one of these two novels.

steps.title: Find the choice that names one of these famous works
steps.content:
The correct choice identifies A Room with a View and calls it "one of C.M. Forster's most famous works," which matches the notes exactly. The general statement that C.M. Forster was an acclaimed novelist does not name a specific famous work. The answers about "Albergo Empedocle" reference the work the notes identify as his first published fiction, not one of his most famous novels.
That confirms C is correct.
